1.1: The word of LORD JEHOVAH that was upon Hosha, son of Beri, in his days of Uzia and of Yotham and of Akhaz and of Khezeqia, Kings of Judea, and in the days of Yorbaam, son of Yoash, King of Israel
1.2: The beginning of the word of LORD JEHOVAH that was upon Hosha: “LORD JEHOVAH said to Hosha: ‘go take to you a wife who commits whoredom and children who commit whoredom because the land of LORD JEHOVAH will surely commit whoredom after him’
1.3: And he went on and he took to him Gomer daughter of Deblim and she conceived and she bore to him a son
1.4: And LORD JEHOVAH said to him ‘call his name Yezreel, because after a little while I avenge the blood of Yezreel against the house of Yehu, and I shall destroy the kingdom of the house of Israel
1.5: In that day I shall break the bow of Israel in the valley of Yezreel’
1.6: And she conceived again and she bore a daughter and LORD JEHOVAH said to me: ‘call her name “La Ethrakhmath” because I do not continue to show compassion on those of the house of Israel but I am certainly removing them
1.7: But on the house of Judea I shall show compassion and I shall save them in LORD JEHOVAH their God. I shall not save them with a bow, neither by the sword, neither by war, neither with horses, nor by horsemen’
1.8: And she weaned La Ethrakhmath and she conceived again and she bore a son
1.9: And LORD JEHOVAH said to me: ‘call his name La Ammi, because you are not my people, and I shall not be yours
1.10: And if the number of the children of Israel shall be as the sand of the sea that is not measured neither is numbered, also it will be in the place that was spoken to them: ‘you are not my people’, there they shall be called the children of The Living God
1.11: And the children of Yehuda and the children of Israel will be assembled as one and they shall make for themselves one Leader and they shall come up from the land for great is the day of Yezreel

2.1: Call your brethren Ammi and your sisters Rakhimtha
2.2: Judge with your mother, judge because she was not my wife, and I have not been her husband; she shall put away her fornication from her face and her adultery from between her breasts
2.3: That I will not strip her naked and I would leave her like the day in which she was born and I would make her as the wilderness and like the thirsty land and I would kill her with thirst
2.4: And upon her children I shall not show compassion because they are children of a whore
2.5: Because their mother has been a whore and she who gave them birth has been shameful and she said ‘I will go after my lovers, givers of my bread and water and my clothing and my linen and my oil, and everything that I require’
2.6: Because of these things, behold, I fence in her ways with thistles and I shall hedge in her paths and she will not find the road
2.7: And she shall go after her lovers and she will not overtake them and she will seek them and she will not find them, and she will say: ‘I shall return, I will go myself to my first husband, because it was better to me than now
2.8: But she did not know that I gave her grain and wine and oil, and silver and gold I multiplied to her, and from it they made Baal
2.9: Because of this I shall return I shall take my grain at its time and my wine at its time, and I shall take my wool and my linen that I gave to her that she would cover her nakedness
2.10: Therefore I shall expose her nakedness before her lovers and no man will save her from my hands
2.11: And I shall remove all of the joy of her feasts and the beginning of her months and her sabbaths and all her feasts
2.12: And I shall destroy her vines and fig trees, of which she said: ‘they are gifts which my lovers gave to me’, and I shall make them a forest, and the animals of the wilderness will devour them
2.13: And I shall issue a charge against her, the days of Baal, when she was placing incense in them and she took her earrings and her pearls and she went after her lovers and she has forgotten me, says LORD JEHOVAH
2.14: Because of these things, behold, I will soothe her and I will lead her to the wilderness and I shall fill in her heart
2.15: And I shall give her vineyards to her from there, and the valley of Akhor that her understanding may be opened and she may be humbled there, like the days of her childhood and like the day in which she went up from the land of Egypt
2.16: And it will be in that day, says LORD JEHOVAH, you shall call me ”My husband”, and you shall not again call me ”My Baal”
2.17: Again I shall take the name of Baal from her mouth, and they will not remember his name
2.18: And I shall covenant with them a covenant in that day with the animals of the wilderness, and with the birds of Heaven, and the creeping things of the Earth, and the bow, and the sword, and I shall obliterate battle from the Earth and I shall settle them in confidence
2.19: And I shall betroth you to me for eternity, and I shall betroth you to me in righteousness, and in judgment, and in favor and in compassion
2.20: And I shall betroth you to me in faithfulness and you shall know LORD JEHOVAH
2.21: And it will be in that day, says LORD JEHOVAH, I shall answer the Heavens and they shall answer the Earth
2.22: And Earth will answer the grain and the wine and the oil, and those will answer Yezreel
2.23: And I shall sow her for myself in the Earth and I shall have compassion on La Ethrakhmath “Not loved”, and I shall call La Ammi –“Not my people” Ammi –“My people”, and he shall call me, Alahee –“My God”

3.1: And LORD JEHOVAH said to me: ”Again, go love a woman, an adulteress, who loves evil things, as LORD JEHOVAH loves the children of Israel, and they turned after other gods, and they loved honey cakes of raisins”
3.2: And I bought her for myself for fifteen silver pieces and for a cor and a half cor of barley
3.3: And I said to her: ”Many days you shall dwell with me; you shall not fornicate and you shall not be for a man, and I shall be yours
3.4: Because for many days the children of Israel will dwell without a King and without a Ruler and without a sacrifice and without an altar and without one wearing an ephod and setting incense
3.5: And after this the children of Israel will return and they shall seek LORD JEHOVAH their God and David their King, and they shall know LORD JEHOVAH and his grace in the last days

4.1: Hear the word of LORD JEHOVAH, children of Israel, because LORD JEHOVAH has a judgment with the inhabitants of the land, because there is no truth, neither grace, nor the knowledge of God in the land
4.2: A curse and lying and murder and theft and adultery have increased, and they have mixed blood with blood
4.3: Because of this the land shall dwell in mourning and all its inhabitants shall mourn, and the beast of the wilderness and the bird of Heaven, also the fish that are in the sea shall be consumed
4.4: Because a man does not judge, neither does he rebuke, and your people dispute like a Priest
4.5: And you have stumbled in the daytime and the Prophet of your people stumbled also in the night and your mother has been silent
4.6: My people is silent because there it has no knowledge, for you have rejected knowledge and I shall reject you from the Priesthood, and you have forgotten the law of your God; I also shall forget your children
4.7: for little according to their multitude, so they sinned, and their honor they exchanged
4.8: They consumed the sin of my people and their souls have offered their evil
4.9: And the Priest was like the people, and I shall visit upon them their ways, and their works I shall pay them
4.10: And they shall eat and they will not be satisfied; they fornicated and they have not increased, because they have forsaken the LORD JEHOVAH
4.11: And they loved fornication, and wine and drunkenness have taken their heart
4.12: My people inquires of its mind and its rod shows him, because the spirit of fornication has seduced them and they have turned from their God
4.13: On tops of the mountains they have done sacrifice and upon the hills they placed sweet spices, under oak or poplar or terebinth, because its shade is excellent, because of this your daughters will commit whoredom and your brides shall commit adultery
4.14: I shall not visit your daughters when they will be whores, neither your brides when they shall commit adultery, because these are mingled with fornicators, and they sacrifice with those going out to the streets; the people without understanding embraces a whore
4.15: But you, Israel, you shall not condemn Yehuda, and you shall not go to Galgala, and you shall not go up to Bayth Awan and you shall not swear: ”Living is LORD JEHOVAH!”
4.16: Because, like a cow that slipped out from a yoke, so Israel slips out, and from now on LORD JEHOVAH will shepherd them as lambs in a wide expanse
4.17: Aphreim is a partner of idols; leave him!
4.18: All of them have committed whoredom and they loved disgrace and fear
4.19: The Spirit shall bind them up with their wings, and they will be ashamed of their altars

5.1: Hear this Priests, and obey, house of Israel, and house of the King, hear, because yours is the judgment, because you have been a trap to the Watchmen and a net that is spread on Tabor
5.2: And hunters who hunt have hid traps, but I shall chastise all of them
5.3: I know Aphreim and Israel is not hidden from me, because Aphreim has now committed whoredom, and Israel is defiled
5.4: Their devices do not let them turn to their God because of the spirit of fornication within them, and they have not known LORD JEHOVAH
5.5: And the honor of Israel shall be humbled before him, and Israel and Aphrim will be subverted in their evil, and Yehuda also will be subverted with them
5.6: With their sheep and with their oxen they shall go to seek LORD JEHOVAH and they shall not find him, because he has separated himself from them
5.7: For they were unfaithful to LORD JEHOVAH, because they gave birth to foreign children
5.8: Sound a trumpet in Ramtha and the horn in Ramtha; cry out Bayth Awan, behind you, Benjamin
5.9: Aphreim shall be for destruction in the day of reproof; in the tribes of Israel I have made known faithfulness
5.10: The Princes of Judea were like those who extend borders; I shall pour out my anger upon them like waters
5.11: Aphreim is oppressed and afflicted in judgment because he was pleased to go after nothing
5.12: And I shall be like a tempest to Aphreim and like a lion to the house of Yehuda
5.13: And Aphreim saw his sicknesses and Yehuda his diseases and Aphreim went on to Assyria and he sent to the King who was great and he not could heal you, and he will not heal you your diseases
5.14: Because I am like a lion to Aphreim and like a whelp of a lion to the house of Yehuda; I shall tear apart and I shall go on and I shall take and there is none to deliver
5.15: I shall return, I shall go to my place until they are guilty and they will seek my face, and in their affliction they will come before my presence

6.1: And they will say: ”We shall return, we shall go to LORD JEHOVAH who struck us, and he shall heal us; and he has broken us and he will bind us up
6.2: And he shall give us life in days, and in the third day he shall raise us up and we shall live before him
6.3: And we shall know to go on to the knowledge of LORD JEHOVAH as the dawn, for his going forth is prepared, and he shall come to us as the rain and like spring rain which waters the land
6.4: What shall I do to you, Aphreim, and what shall I do to you, Yehuda? Your goodness is as a cloud of the morning and like the early dew, and it passes away
6.5: Because of this, I have cut off the Prophets and I have killed them with the word of my mouth, and my judgment will come forth as the light
6.6: Because I have pleasure in goodness and not in sacrifice, and in the knowledge of God more than burning peace offerings
6.7: And those, as a son of man, passed over my covenant, and there they were unfaithful to me
6.8: Gelad the city is she who works evil, she wallows in blood, and your strength is like that of a strongman robber
6.9: The Priests cooperated in the way and they murdered Shecheem, because they have done evil
6.10: In the house of Israel I have seen a stupefying wonder; there Aphreim committed whoredom and Israel was defiled
6.11: Also Yehuda, make for yourself a harvest whenever I turn around the captivity of my people

7.1: Whenever I have healed Israel and the evil of Aphreim was revealed and the evil of Samaria, because they had practiced falsehood before me, and the thief was entering and sent a band of robbers into the streets
7.2: And they did not say in their heart that I remember all of their evils, therefore their devices that have been before my face will surround them
7.3: They gave joy to Kings and in their evil and in their unfaithfulness
7.4: All their Rulers are adulterers as an oven burning for baking; one desists from the city; he kneads and kneads until it is leavened
7.5: The day that they give counsel, the Princes begin to be passionate from wine; they reach out their hands with evil ones
7.6: Because their heart is hot like a furnace in their ambush, and all night their passion grew old, and at dawn it was burning like fire and a flame
7.7: All of them were hot like a furnace and they have consumed their Judges; all their Kings have fallen and there is none among them who called to me
7.8: Aphreim is mixed among the Gentiles; Aphreim was a cake; before it was baked it was eaten
7.9: In this way foreigners have consumed his power, and he did not know, and white hairs have come out on him and he did not know it
7.10: And the honor of Israel was laid low before him and they did not turn to LORD JEHOVAH their God, and they did not look for him
7.11: And Aphreim was like a baby dove; there is no heart in him; they came to Egypt and they went to Assyria
7.12: I shall spread upon them my net in the place where they will go, and like a bird of Heaven I shall bring them down and I shall chastise them according to the report of their testimony
7.13: Woe to them who have turned away from me! I shall bring evil upon them because they have done evil to me, for I have redeemed them and they have spoken falsehood against me
7.14: And they have not cried to me from all their heart, but they wailed upon their bed for the grain and they were contending for wine, and they provoked me
7.15: Against me and I have trained and I have strengthened their arms, and they have conceived evil
7.16: They have perverted themselves for nothing and they were like a deceitful bow; their Princes shall fall by the sword by the audacity of their tongue; this is their entanglement of that in the land of Egypt

8.1: Your mouth like a trumpet and like an eagle came into the house of LORD JEHOVAH because they violated my covenant and they committed evil against my Law
8.2: “To me they called and they said: ”We know you, our God!”
8.3: Israel has forgotten goodness and the enemy chases them
8.4: They took counsel, but not from me, and they were authorized and they did not inform me. Their silver and their gold they have made idols for themselves that they would be destroyed
8.5: They have strayed by your calf, Samaria, and my anger was provoked against them; how long are they unable to be innocent?
8.6: Because it is from Israel and a Carpenter made it and it is not God, but your calf was for a deception, Samaria
8.7: Because they sowed wind and they have reaped a hurricane, and they do not have a stalk, neither an ear that makes meal, and if they had foreigners, they would have been eating it
8.8: Israel is swallowed up, and from now on they are among the Gentiles like a vessel with no usefulness in it
8.9: Gifts because they came up to Assyria like a lone donkey; Aphreim has loved
8.10: Even if they are handed over among the Gentiles, I shall gather them and they shall rest a little from the burden of the Kings and of the Rulers
8.11: Because Aphreim has multiplied altars for sin, and he had altars for great sin
8.12: And I have written to him the abundance of my laws, and he considered my words as a foreign woman
8.13: They sacrifice the sacrifices of the chosen ones and they eat the flesh, and LORD JEHOVAH is not pleased with them, therefore their evil will be remembered and he shall review their sins and they shall return to Egypt
8.14: Israel has forgotten The One who made him, and built temples, and Yehuda multiplied fortress cities. I shall set a fire in his city and it will devour her palaces

9.1: You, Israel, shall not rejoice, and you shall not dance like the nations, because you have strayed from your God and you loved gifts from every granary
9.2: And from the granary and from the winepress they will not be satisfied, and the oil will fail among them
9.3: They shall not dwell in the land of LORD JEHOVAH, but Aphreim shall return to Egypt, and in Assyria they shall eat defilement
9.4: They shall not pour out wine to LORD JEHOVAH, and their sacrifices will not be sweet to him, as it is the bread of affliction to them; all eating it shall be defiled because the bread of their souls does not enter the house of LORD JEHOVAH
9.5: What will you do for the day of the feast and for the day of the feast of LORD JEHOVAH?
9.6: Because those went into the spoil and Egypt shall collect them and Mephes shall bury them; foreigners shall inherit the lust of their silver and the thorns in their tents
9.7: The days of vengeance have arrived; the days of payment have approached; Israel shall know the Prophet is a fool; a man is a madman for the evil in him; the spirit of insanity from the abundance of your evil made your debauchery to super abound
9.8: The Watchman of Aphreim was with my God; the Prophet is a snare of subversion upon all his ways, and debauchery in the house of God
9.9: They have dug down deep and they have become corrupt as the days of Ramtha, therefore their evil will be remembered and he shall judge their sins
9.10: Like grapes in the wilderness I found Israel, and like a fig tree first fruits I have seen their fathers, and they entered to Baal Peor and they separated themselves to shame and they became an abomination like that which they loved
9.11: Aphreim like a bird has flown away, their glory from birth, and from the womb and from conception
9.12: Even if they shall raise children I shall bereave them of children of men, because woe to them, I have vengeance of them!
9.13: Aphreim, as I have seen at Tyre which was planted among its buildings, thus Aphreim sends out his sons for slaughter
9.14: Give them, LORD JEHOVAH, the thing that you give them: give them a barren womb and dry breasts
9.15: All their evils in Galgala, because there I have hated them; in the evil of their strategies of my house I shall send them out and I shall not continue to show love to them. All their Rulers are rebellious
9.16: Aphreim struck a root and it dried up that it will not give fruit, and if they bear children, I shall put to death the lust of their loins
9.17: And my God shall reject them, because they did not listen to him, and they shall be wandering among the nations

10.1: A vine of shoots, Israel, that has produced fruit according to the abundance of his fruit; they have multiplied altars and according to the blessing of their land they have built high places
10.2: Their heart has been divided and from now they will be condemned; he will overthrow their altars and he shall plunder their high places
10.3: Because from now they will say: ”We have no King from LORD JEHOVAH; we do not stand in awe of a King! What shall he do for us?”
10.4: They have spoken words of false harvests; they have cut off the covenant and they paid judgment like a tangle of thorns in a field of barren land
10.5: The calf of Bayth Awan shall possess the settlers, dwellers of Samaria, because his people and his Priests dwelt upon it in mourning and they will rejoice over it and over his honor that has passed from it
10.6: Also they shall bring a gift to Assyria to the King who is great; Aphreim will receive shame and Israel will be ashamed of his plan
10.7: Samaria sent her King like a chip on the face of the waters
10.8: And the idol shrines of Awan, the sin of Israel, shall be destroyed . Thorns and thistles shall grow in their altars and they will say to the mountains: "Cover us”, and to the hills, that they would fall upon them
10.9: From the days of Ramtha you have sinned, Israel; there they will remain and war shall not overtake them in Ramtha against the children of evil
10.10: In my rebuke I shall chastise them and the nations will be assembled against them when they are chastised for their two offenses
10.11: Aphreim is a trained heifer that loves to tread and I passed over his neck; I will mount Aphreim and he will tread Yehuda and shall plunder Yaqob
10.12: Sow to yourselves righteousness and reap the mouth of grace; light up for yourselves a lamp because it is the time to seek LORD JEHOVAH until he comes and shows to you his righteousness
10.13: You have ploughed sin and evil and you have harvested and you have eaten the fruits of lying because you have trusted in your ways and in the abundance of your might
10.14: Destruction shall rise among your people and all around you shall be plundered as loot that is handed over from Bayth Eil; in the day of war the mother with her children were dashed in pieces
10.15: Thus they did to you, Bayth Eil, from before the wickedness of your wickedness at dawn; the King of Israel was shocked and was ashamed

11.1: Because when Israel was a child, I loved him, and from Egypt I called him my son
11.2: For as they called them thus they went from before me and they made sacrifice to Baal and they placed incense for carved images
11.3: And I led Aphreim and I took them on my arms and they have not known that I have healed them
11.4: With the cords of the children of men I pulled them and with the chains of love, and I have been to them as he who takes a yoke from their neck and I stooped to them and they ate
11.5: They shall not return to the land of Egypt, and an Assyrian is their King, because they chose not to return
11.6: The sword shall cause distress in their cities and it will vanish from their hands, and they shall eat of their counsels
11.7: And my nation depends on dwelling with me and to God they will call and it will think as one and it shall not be exalted
11.8: How shall I support you Aphreim, or how shall I help you Israel, or how shall I make you as Adma or as Tseboim? My heart has been turned and my compassions within
11.9: I shall not execute the heat of my wrath and I shall not return to destroy Aphreim, because I am God and I have not been a son of man among you. I am The Holy One and I shall not attack the city
11.10: They shall go after LORD JEHOVAH as a lion that roars, because he shall roar and the children of the people shall tremble
11.11: And they shall be stirred up like a bird of prey from Egypt and like a dove from the land of Assyria, and I shall lead them to their dwelling, says LORD JEHOVAH
11.12: Aphreim and the house of Israel and Yehuda surrounded me with lies in treachery, until he would bring down the people of God, the holy and faithful people

12.1: Aphreim feeds on the wind and pursues a hurricane all day; they have multiplied falsehood and spoil, and they have cut a covenant with the Assyrians, and they have brought oil to Egypt
12.2: And LORD JEHOVAH has a judgment with Yehuda and will summon Yaqob according to his ways, and he shall pay him according to his devices
12.3: For he deceived his brother in the womb and in his strength he was made great before God
12.4: And he was a match for the power of an Angel and he begged of him in Bayth Eil; he found him and there he spoke with him
12.5: And LORD JEHOVAH God of Hosts commemorated him
12.6: But you, return to your God, and keep grace and judgment; expect your God constantly
12.7: A scale of deceit is in the hand of Canaan; he loves to oppress
12.8: And Aphreim said: ”I have become rich and I have found for myself sorrow, and all my labor is not enough to me for the sin that I have sinned”
12.9: I AM LORD JEHOVAH your God who brought you out from the land of Egypt; I shall settle you again in tents like the days of the feasts
12.10: And I have spoken with the Prophets and I have multiplied my visions and by the agency of the Prophets I have been been represented
12.11: In Gelad there was pain and in Galgala you sacrificed bulls for futility, also your altars were like straw in a field of barren land
12.12: And Yaqob fled to the land of Aram and Israel worked for the sake of a wife, and he kept watch for the sake of a wife
12.13: And by the Prophets LORD JEHOVAH brought up Israel from Egypt and by the Prophets it was preserved
12.14: Aphreim was angry and bitter and his blood shall be poured upon him and his Lord will return his shame to him

13.1: When Aphreim was speaking he was trembling, and he was great in Israel, and he was condemned in Baal and he died
13.2: And now they have added to sin and they have made for themselves a molten image from their silver in their likeness, an idol, the work of a Carpenter, and they were saying to them: ”Sacrifice children of men and kiss the calf”
13.3: Because of this, they shall be as a cloud of the morning and like dew that is early, and it passed by and like a straw that flies from the granary and like smoke from a window
13.4: I AM LORD JEHOVAH your God who brought you out from the land of Egypt; you shall not know a god outside of me and there is none who saves but I
13.5: I shepherded you in the wilderness in a desolate land without inhabitant
13.6: And I fed them and they filled their belly, and their heart was lifted up; because of this they have forgotten me
13.7: And I have been to them like a lion and like a leopard on the road of Assyria
13.8: I shall meet with them like a bear that is wounded and I shall seek the membrane of their heart, and a lion will eat them there, and a wild beast shall tear them to pieces
13.9: You have corrupted yourselves, Israel; who will help you?
13.10: Where is, therefore, your King? Let him save you and all your cities, and your Judge whom you asked of me. And you said: ”Give me a King and a Ruler!”
13.11: And I gave you a King in my passion, and I have taken him in my anger
13.12: The evil of Aphreim is bound up and his sin is secret
13.13: Pains as of a woman in labor will come upon him; because he is a son that is not wise, therefore he shall not remain in the pains of bearing children
13.14: From the hand of Sheol I shall redeem them, and from death I shall save them. Where is therefore your victory, death? Oh, where is your sting, Sheol? Comfort is hidden from my eyes
13.15: Because he shall separate between brothers, the East wind of LORD JEHOVAH will come from the wilderness; it shall come up and shall destroy its wells and shall dry up its springs, and he will plunder the treasury of all vessels of desire
13.16: Samaria shall be condemned because she was embittered against God; by the sword they shall fall and their infants shall be dashed in pieces and their pregnant women shall be ripped up

14.1: Turn, Israel, to LORD JEHOVAH your God, because you have been subverted by your evil
14.2: Take for yourselves words and be converted to LORD JEHOVAH your God and speak to him that he may forgive you your evil, and receive good, and he shall reward you the fruit of your lips
14.3: And say: ”The Assyrians will not save us and upon horses we will not ride, and we will not again call the work of our hands gods, because you are merciful to orphans”
14.4: I shall heal their backsliding and I shall love their vows and shall turn back my anger from them
14.5: And I shall be like dew to Israel and he will bud as a lily, and he shall put out his roots like those of Lebanon
14.6: And he shall send out his branches and he will be like an olive tree that is lovely with its fruits, and its scent like that of Lebanon
14.7: They shall be restored, they shall dwell in his shadow and they shall live from the grain and they shall be fruitful like a vine and their memorial like the wine of Lebanon
14.8: And Aphreim shall say: ”What more have I to do with idols?” And I have humbled him, and I have found him as an almond tree thickly grown, also from me your fruits are found
14.9: Whoever is wise, let him perceive these things and consider to know them, that the ways of LORD JEHOVAH are right, and the righteous ones walk in them and the evil stumble in them
